title: larix decidua subsp. sibirica
intro: Larix decidua subsp. sibirica, also known as Siberian larch, is a subspecies of the larch tree, native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia and Eastern Europe. The bark of the Siberian larch has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. Siberian larch is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to take advantage of its medicinal properties and health benefits.
